---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Carlos"
date: "2026-04-15"
output: pdf_document
---


```{r setup,include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE,error = F, message = F, warning = F,comment = "")
```


```{r}

rm(list = ls())
library(BGLR)
library(readr)
library(nortest)
library(coda)
library(moments)
library(HDInterval)

setwd("C:/Users/karre/Downloads/GBLUP-R-Rendimiento")

Dat <- read.csv("Conventional_Long_Traits_2018_24_Shared_updatedwithchecks_FINALCLEANED.csv",
  header = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

load("C:/Users/karre/Downloads/X.RData")



```


```{r,echo=FALSE,include=FALSE}
# Aqui elimino la fila que contiene encabezado.
Dat <- Dat[Dat$V1 != "germplasmName", ]

```


# 24
```{r}
# =========================================================
# 1. Filtrar Datos: Predecir 2024 
# =========================================================
Ren <- Dat[Dat$V7 == "Yield.LSU_01.0000138", ]

# Entrenamiento: Años históricos (2018-2023)
R_train <- Ren[Ren$V6 %in% 2018:2023, ]
# Test: El futuro (2024)
R_test  <- Ren[Ren$V6 == 2024, ]

# =========================================================
# 2. Fenotipos y IDs (Sincronización Cruda)
# =========================================================
y_train_raw <- as.numeric(R_train$V5)/1000
y_test_raw  <- as.numeric(R_test$V5)/1000

id_train_raw <- trimws(as.character(R_train$V1))
id_test_raw  <- trimws(as.character(R_test$V1))

# Identificar quiénes SÍ tienen datos en la matriz X
keep_train <- id_train_raw %in% rownames(X)
keep_test  <- id_test_raw  %in% rownames(X)

# Filtrado sincronizado de IDs y Fenotipos
id_train <- id_train_raw[keep_train]
id_test  <- id_test_raw[keep_test]

y_train <- y_train_raw[keep_train]
y_test  <- y_test_raw[keep_test]

# Extraer Matrices X
X_train <- X[id_train, , drop = FALSE]
X_test  <- X[id_test, , drop = FALSE]

# =========================================================
# 3. Construcción de Matrices de Relación (GBLUP)
# =========================================================
# 1. Centra el entrenamiento 
mu_train <- colMeans(X_train)
Xc_train <- scale(X_train, center = mu_train, scale = FALSE)

# 2. Centra el test usando EL MISMO centro del entrenamiento
Xc_test  <- scale(X_test, center = mu_train, scale = FALSE)

# 3. Crea Matrices K (Relación Genómica)
K_train      <- tcrossprod(Xc_train) / ncol(Xc_train)
K_test_train <- (Xc_test %*% t(Xc_train)) / ncol(Xc_train)

# =========================================================
# 4. Ajuste del Modelo BGLR (RKHS)

#year_train <- factor(R_train$V6[keep_train])

#X_year <- model.matrix(~ year_train)[, -1, drop = FALSE]



year_train <- factor(R_train$V6[keep_train])
X_year <- model.matrix(~ 0 + year_train)

# Crear matriz de incidencia para efectos aleatorios (Simetría total)


ETA <- list(
  ENV = list(X = X_year, model = "BRR"),
  G   = list(K = K_train, model = "RKHS")
)


# =========================================================
#ETA <- list(list(K = K_train, model = "RKHS"))

set.seed(12)
fm_R <- BGLR(y = y_train, ETA = ETA, nIter = 55000, burnIn = 40000, 
             saveAt = "pred24_c1_", verbose = FALSE)

set.seed(13)
fm2_R <- BGLR(y = y_train, ETA = ETA, nIter = 55000, burnIn = 40000, 
              saveAt = "pred24_c2_", verbose = FALSE)
str(fm_R)
# =========================================================
# 5. Predicción para 2024
# =========================================================
library(MASS)

# Promedio de las dos cadenas (Ensemble)
u_hat_train <- (fm_R$ETA[[2]]$u + fm2_R$ETA[[2]]$u) / 2

#u_hat_train <- (fm_R$ETA[[1]]$u + fm2_R$ETA[[1]]$u) / 2
mu_hat      <- (fm_R$mu + fm2_R$mu) / 2


# BLUP para el año 2024 (Test) usando la matriz cruzada
u_hat_2024<- K_test_train %*% ginv(K_train) %*% u_hat_train
pred_2024_total <- as.vector(u_hat_2024 + mu_hat)
#########################################################################################################################################################

SelectionStatistics <- function(y_test,pred_2024_total,p=0.10){

    datos <- data.frame(
        Observed=y_test,
        Predicted=pred_2024_total
    )

    datos <- datos[
        order(datos$Predicted,decreasing=TRUE),
    ]

    nTop <- ceiling(p*nrow(datos))

    top <- datos[1:nTop,]

    MeanPopulation <- mean(datos$Observed)

    MeanTop <- mean(top$Observed)

    SD <- MeanTop-MeanPopulation

    SDpercent <- 100*SD/MeanPopulation

    list(
        MeanPopulation=MeanPopulation,
        MeanTop=MeanTop,
        SelectionDifferential=SD,
        SelectionDifferentialPercent=SDpercent,
        TopIndividuals=top
    )
}

GBLUP <- SelectionStatistics(y_test,pred_2024_total)

GBLUP$TopIndividuals



# =========================================================
# 6. Resultados y Gráfico
# =========================================================
cor_2024 <- cor(y_test, pred_2024_total, use = "complete.obs")
rmse_2024 <- sqrt(mean((y_test - pred_2024_total)^2))

cat("\n--- RESULTADOS GBLUP 2024 ---\n")
cat("Correlación:", round(cor_2024, 4), "\n")
cat("RMSE:", round(rmse_2024, 4), "\n")

plot(y_test, pred_2024_total, col = "red", pch = 19,
     xlab = "Observado 2024", ylab = "Predicho GBLUP",
     main = paste("GBLUP 2024 - Cor:", round(cor_2024, 3)))
abline(0, 1, col = "blue", lwd = 2, lty = 2)











png("GBLUP_Validation_2024_HD.png", 
    width = 1800, height = 1800, res = 300) 

# 2. Configurar parámetros gráficos: pty="s" asegura que sea cuadrado
par(pty = "s", mar = c(5, 5, 4, 2)) 

# 3. Crear el gráfico base (IMPORTANTE: axes = FALSE)
plot(y_test, pred_2024_total, 
     xlab = "Observed ",   
     ylab = "Predicted ",  
     pch = 19, 
     col = "darkred",
     cex.lab = 1.1,    
     axes = FALSE) # Esto permite que luego definamos los ejes a mano

# 4. Dibujar los ejes de forma remarcada
axis(1, lwd = 2) # Eje X con línea gruesa
axis(2, lwd = 2) # Eje Y con línea gruesa

# 5. Añadir la caja en forma de "L" (remarca los ejes X e Y)
box(bty = "l", lwd = 2)

# 6. Añadir la línea de referencia 1:1 (discontinua)
abline(0, 1, col = "blue", lwd = 2, lty = 2)

# 7. Cerrar y guardar el archivo
dev.off()

cat("Imagen generada exitosamente en tu carpeta de trabajo.")
```





# Posterior
```{r}
# =========================================================

# =========================================================
library(coda)
library(bayesplot)
library(ggplot2)

cols <- c("#FF0FFF", "#00BFFF", "#32CD32", "#FF0000")

load_mcmc_named <- function(pattern, par_name) {
  c1 <- scan(paste0("pred24_c1_", pattern), quiet = TRUE)
  c2 <- scan(paste0("pred24_c2_", pattern), quiet = TRUE)
  
 
  mcmc.list(
    mcmc(matrix(c1, ncol = 1, dimnames = list(NULL, par_name))),
    mcmc(matrix(c2, ncol = 1, dimnames = list(NULL, par_name)))
  )
}


mu_mcmc  <- load_mcmc_named("mu.dat", "Global_Mean")
u_mcmc   <- load_mcmc_named("ETA_G_varU.dat", "Additive_Variance_G")
e_mcmc   <- load_mcmc_named("varE.dat", "Residual_Variance_E")
env_mcmc <- load_mcmc_named("ETA_ENV_varB.dat","Varianza ambiental")

str(fm_R)

mu_com <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, mu_mcmc))
u_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, u_mcmc))
e_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, e_mcmc))
env_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, env_mcmc))

params_list <- list(mu_mcmc, u_mcmc, e_mcmc,env_mcmc)
file_names   <- c("Trace_Mean", "Trace_VarG", "Trace_VarE", "Trace_VarEnv")


plot_titles <- list(
  expression(mu), 
  expression(sigma[G]^2), 
  expression(sigma[e]^2),
  expression(sigma[env]^2)
)

# =========================================================
# 3. BUCLE PARA GUARDAR TRAZAS HD
# =========================================================
for(i in 1:length(params_list)){
  p <- mcmc_trace(params_list[[i]]) + 
    ggtitle(plot_titles[[i]]) +  # Doble corchete para la expresión
    scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
    theme_classic() + 
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none",
      plot.title = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0.5)
    ) +
    labs(x = "Iteration", y = "Value")
  
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(filename = paste0(file_names[i], ".tiff"), plot = p,
         width = 8, height = 4.5, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
}


```


#HDI

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 5. HDI ANALYSIS (CORRECTED FOR FULL CHAINS)
# =========================================================
library(HDInterval)
mu_real_chain  <- (mu_com ) 
u_real_chain   <- u_com  
e_real_chain   <- e_com 
env_real_chain   <- env_com 

h2_chain <- (mu_com + u_com+env_com ) / (mu_com + u_com + env_com  + e_com)




summary(h2_chain)
# 3. Función de cálculo (Mediana y HDI 95%)
calc_real_stats <- function(chain) {
  h <- hdi(chain, credMass = 0.95)
  return(c(Median = median(chain), Lower = h[1], Upper = h[2]))
}

# 4. Aplicar a las cadenas
summary_table <- rbind(
  `Global Mean (mu)`  = calc_real_stats(mu_real_chain),
  `Variance G`        = calc_real_stats(u_real_chain),
  `Variance E`        = calc_real_stats(e_real_chain),
   `Variance Env`        = calc_real_stats(env_real_chain),
  `Heritability (h2)` = calc_real_stats(h2_chain)
)

colnames(summary_table) <- c("Posterior Median", "95% HDI Lower", "95% HDI Upper")

cat("\n========== CORRECTED POSTERIOR SUMMARY (REAL SCALE) ==========\n")
print(round(summary_table, 4))

summary(h2_chain)
```




# Gelma-Rubin
```{r}
# =========================================================
# 6. GELMAN-RUBIN 
# =========================================================
library(coda)

# Calcular el diagnóstico para cada parámetro
# Un valor cercano a 1.00 (ej. < 1.1) indica buena convergencia
gr_mu  <- gelman.diag(mu_mcmc)
gr_u   <- gelman.diag(u_mcmc)
gr_e   <- gelman.diag(e_mcmc)
gr_env <- gelman.diag(env_mcmc)

gr_table <- rbind(
  `Global Mean (mu)`   = gr_mu$psrf[1, ],
  `Variance G`         = gr_u$psrf[1, ],
  `Variance E`         = gr_e$psrf[1, ],
   `Variance Env`         = gr_env$psrf[1, ]
)

colnames(gr_table) <- c("Point Est. (R-hat)", "Upper C.I.")

cat("\n========== GELMAN-RUBIN DIAGNOSTIC ==========\n")
cat("Values close to 1.00 indicate convergence.\n\n")
print(round(gr_table, 4))
cat("=============================================\n")


max_rhat <- max(gr_table[, 1])
if(max_rhat < 1.1) {
  cat("Success: All parameters have converged (R-hat < 1.1).\n")
} else {
  cat("Warning: Some parameters may not have converged. Consider increasing nIter.\n")
}
```




# 23

```{r}

Ren <- Dat[Dat$V7 == "Yield.LSU_01.0000138", ]

R_train <- Ren[Ren$V6 %in% c(2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2024), ]
# Test: Solo el año 2023
R_test  <- Ren[Ren$V6 == 2023, ]

# =========================================================
# 2. Fenotipos y IDs
# =========================================================
y_train_raw <- as.numeric(R_train$V5)/1000
y_test_raw  <- as.numeric(R_test$V5)/1000

id_train_raw <- trimws(as.character(R_train$V1))
id_test_raw  <- trimws(as.character(R_test$V1))

# Sincronización con Matriz X
keep_train <- id_train_raw %in% rownames(X)
keep_test  <- id_test_raw  %in% rownames(X)

id_train <- id_train_raw[keep_train]
id_test  <- id_test_raw[keep_test]

y_train <- y_train_raw[keep_train]
y_test  <- y_test_raw[keep_test]

# Matrices X
X_train <- X[id_train, , drop = FALSE]
X_test  <- X[id_test, , drop = FALSE]

# =========================================================
# 3. Construcción de Matrices de Relación (GBLUP)
# =========================================================
# Centrar entrenamiento y guardar mu para el test
mu_train <- colMeans(X_train)
Xc_train <- scale(X_train, center = mu_train, scale = FALSE)
Xc_test  <- scale(X_test, center = mu_train, scale = FALSE)

# Matriz K entrenamiento
K_train <- tcrossprod(Xc_train) / ncol(Xc_train)
# Matriz K test-train
K_test_train <- (Xc_test %*% t(Xc_train)) / ncol(Xc_train)

# =========================================================
# 4. Ajuste del Modelo BGLR (RKHS)

#year_train <- factor(R_train$V6[keep_train])

#X_year <- model.matrix(~ year_train)[, -1, drop = FALSE]


year_train <- factor(R_train$V6[keep_train])
X_year <- model.matrix(~ 0 + year_train)



ETA <- list(
  ENV = list(X = X_year, model = "BRR"),
  G   = list(K = K_train, model = "RKHS")
)
# =========================================================
#ETA <- list(list(K = K_train, model = "RKHS"))

set.seed(12)
fm_R <- BGLR(y = y_train, ETA = ETA, nIter = 70000, burnIn = 55000, 
             saveAt = "pred23_c1_", verbose = FALSE)

set.seed(13)
fm2_R <- BGLR(y = y_train, ETA = ETA, nIter = 70000, burnIn = 55000, 
              saveAt = "pred23_c2_", verbose = FALSE)



str(fm_R)
# =========================================================
# 5. Predicción para 2023
# =========================================================
library(MASS)

# Promedio de efectos aleatorios y media global

u_hat_train <- (fm_R$ETA[[2]]$u + fm2_R$ETA[[2]]$u) / 2

#u_hat_train <- (fm_R$ETA[[1]]$u + fm2_R$ETA[[1]]$u) / 2
mu_hat      <- (fm_R$mu + fm2_R$mu) / 2

# BLUP para el año 2023 (Test)
u_hat_2023      <- K_test_train %*% ginv(K_train) %*% u_hat_train
pred_2023_total <- as.vector(u_hat_2023 + mu_hat)

# =========================================================
# 6. Resultados y Gráfico
# =========================================================
cor_2023 <- cor(y_test, pred_2023_total, use = "complete.obs")
rmse_2023 <- sqrt(mean((y_test - pred_2023_total)^2))

cat("Correlación 2023:", round(cor_2023, 4), "\n")
cat("RMSE 2023:", round(rmse_2023, 4), "\n")

plot(y_test, pred_2023_total, col = "blue", pch = 19,
     xlab = "Observado 2023", ylab = "Predicho GBLUP",
     main = paste("Predicción 2023 - Cor:", round(cor_2023, 3)))
abline(0, 1, col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
```

# Posterior
```{r}
# =========================================================

# =========================================================
library(coda)
library(bayesplot)
library(ggplot2)

cols <- c("#FF0FFF", "#00BFFF", "#32CD32", "#FF0000")

load_mcmc_named <- function(pattern, par_name) {
  c1 <- scan(paste0("pred23_c1_", pattern), quiet = TRUE)
  c2 <- scan(paste0("pred23_c2_", pattern), quiet = TRUE)
  
 
  mcmc.list(
    mcmc(matrix(c1, ncol = 1, dimnames = list(NULL, par_name))),
    mcmc(matrix(c2, ncol = 1, dimnames = list(NULL, par_name)))
  )
}


mu_mcmc  <- load_mcmc_named("mu.dat", "Global_Mean")
u_mcmc   <- load_mcmc_named("ETA_G_varU.dat", "Additive_Variance_G")
e_mcmc   <- load_mcmc_named("varE.dat", "Residual_Variance_E")
env_mcmc <- load_mcmc_named("ETA_ENV_varB.dat","Varianza ambiental")

str(fm_R)

mu_com <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, mu_mcmc))
u_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, u_mcmc))
e_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, e_mcmc))
env_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, env_mcmc))

params_list <- list(mu_mcmc, u_mcmc, e_mcmc,env_mcmc)
file_names   <- c("Trace_Mean", "Trace_VarG", "Trace_VarE", "Trace_VarEnv")


plot_titles <- list(
  expression(mu), 
  expression(sigma[G]^2), 
  expression(sigma[e]^2),
  expression(sigma[env]^2)
)

# =========================================================
# 3. BUCLE PARA GUARDAR TRAZAS HD
# =========================================================
for(i in 1:length(params_list)){
  p <- mcmc_trace(params_list[[i]]) + 
    ggtitle(plot_titles[[i]]) +  # Doble corchete para la expresión
    scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
    theme_classic() + 
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none",
      plot.title = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0.5)
    ) +
    labs(x = "Iteration", y = "Value")
  
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(filename = paste0(file_names[i], ".tiff"), plot = p,
         width = 8, height = 4.5, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
}


```


#HDI

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 5. HDI ANALYSIS (CORRECTED FOR FULL CHAINS)
# =========================================================
library(HDInterval)
mu_real_chain  <- (mu_com ) 
u_real_chain   <- u_com  
e_real_chain   <- e_com 
env_real_chain   <- env_com 

h2_chain <- (mu_com + u_com+env_com ) / (mu_com + u_com + env_com  + e_com)




summary(h2_chain)
# 3. Función de cálculo (Mediana y HDI 95%)
calc_real_stats <- function(chain) {
  h <- hdi(chain, credMass = 0.95)
  return(c(Median = median(chain), Lower = h[1], Upper = h[2]))
}

# 4. Aplicar a las cadenas
summary_table <- rbind(
  `Global Mean (mu)`  = calc_real_stats(mu_real_chain),
  `Variance G`        = calc_real_stats(u_real_chain),
  `Variance E`        = calc_real_stats(e_real_chain),
   `Variance Env`        = calc_real_stats(env_real_chain),
  `Heritability (h2)` = calc_real_stats(h2_chain)
)

colnames(summary_table) <- c("Posterior Median", "95% HDI Lower", "95% HDI Upper")

cat("\n========== CORRECTED POSTERIOR SUMMARY (REAL SCALE) ==========\n")
print(round(summary_table, 4))

summary(h2_chain)
```




# Gelma-Rubin
```{r}
# =========================================================
# 6. GELMAN-RUBIN 
# =========================================================
library(coda)

# Calcular el diagnóstico para cada parámetro
# Un valor cercano a 1.00 (ej. < 1.1) indica buena convergencia
gr_mu  <- gelman.diag(mu_mcmc)
gr_u   <- gelman.diag(u_mcmc)
gr_e   <- gelman.diag(e_mcmc)
gr_env <- gelman.diag(env_mcmc)

gr_table <- rbind(
  `Global Mean (mu)`   = gr_mu$psrf[1, ],
  `Variance G`         = gr_u$psrf[1, ],
  `Variance E`         = gr_e$psrf[1, ],
   `Variance Env`         = gr_env$psrf[1, ]
)

colnames(gr_table) <- c("Point Est. (R-hat)", "Upper C.I.")

cat("\n========== GELMAN-RUBIN DIAGNOSTIC ==========\n")
cat("Values close to 1.00 indicate convergence.\n\n")
print(round(gr_table, 4))
cat("=============================================\n")


max_rhat <- max(gr_table[, 1])
if(max_rhat < 1.1) {
  cat("Success: All parameters have converged (R-hat < 1.1).\n")
} else {
  cat("Warning: Some parameters may not have converged. Consider increasing nIter.\n")
}
```




# 2022

```{r}

Ren <- Dat[Dat$V7 == "Yield.LSU_01.0000138", ]


R_train <- Ren[Ren$V6 %in% c(2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2023, 2024), ]

R_test  <- Ren[Ren$V6 == 2022, ]
# =========================================================
y_train_raw <- as.numeric(R_train$V5)/1000
y_test_raw  <- as.numeric(R_test$V5)/1000

id_train_raw <- trimws(as.character(R_train$V1))
id_test_raw  <- trimws(as.character(R_test$V1))

# Sincronización con Matriz X
keep_train <- id_train_raw %in% rownames(X)
keep_test  <- id_test_raw  %in% rownames(X)

id_train <- id_train_raw[keep_train]
id_test  <- id_test_raw[keep_test]

y_train <- y_train_raw[keep_train]
y_test  <- y_test_raw[keep_test]

# Matrices X
X_train <- X[id_train, , drop = FALSE]
X_test  <- X[id_test, , drop = FALSE]

# =========================================================
# Construcción de Matrices de Relación (GBLUP)

mu_train <- colMeans(X_train)
Xc_train <- scale(X_train, center = mu_train, scale = FALSE)
Xc_test  <- scale(X_test, center = mu_train, scale = FALSE)

# Matriz K entrenamiento
K_train <- tcrossprod(Xc_train) / ncol(Xc_train)
# Matriz K test-train
K_test_train <- (Xc_test %*% t(Xc_train)) / ncol(Xc_train)

# =========================================================
# 4. Ajuste del Modelo BGLR (RKHS)
year_train <- factor(R_train$V6[keep_train])
X_year <- model.matrix(~ 0 + year_train)

ETA <- list(
  ENV = list(X = X_year, model = "BRR"),
  G   = list(K = K_train, model = "RKHS")
)
# =========================================================
#ETA <- list(list(K = K_train, model = "RKHS"))

set.seed(12)
fm_R <- BGLR(y = y_train, ETA = ETA, nIter = 75000, burnIn = 55000, 
             saveAt = "pred22_c1_", verbose = TRUE)

set.seed(13)
fm2_R <- BGLR(y = y_train, ETA = ETA, nIter = 75000, burnIn = 55000, 
              saveAt = "pred22_c2_", verbose = TRUE)

# =========================================================
# =========================================================
library(MASS)

# Promedio de efectos aleatorios y media global
u_hat_train <- (fm_R$ETA[[2]]$u + fm2_R$ETA[[2]]$u) / 2
mu_hat      <- (fm_R$mu + fm2_R$mu) / 2

u_hat_2022      <- K_test_train %*% ginv(K_train) %*% u_hat_train
pred_2022_total <- as.vector(u_hat_2022 + mu_hat)


# =========================================================
# 6. Resultados y Gráfico
# =========================================================
cor_2022 <- cor(y_test, pred_2022_total, use = "complete.obs")
rmse_2022 <- sqrt(mean((y_test - pred_2022_total)^2))

cat("Correlación 2022:", round(cor_2022, 4), "\n")
cat("RMSE 2022:", round(rmse_2022, 4), "\n")

plot(y_test, pred_2022_total, col = "blue", pch = 19,
     xlab = "Observado 2022", ylab = "Predicho GBLUP",
     main = paste("Predicción 2022 - Cor:", round(cor_2022, 3)))
abline(0, 1, col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
```


# Posterior
```{r}
# =========================================================

# =========================================================
library(coda)
library(bayesplot)
library(ggplot2)

cols <- c("#FF0FFF", "#00BFFF", "#32CD32", "#FF0000")

load_mcmc_named <- function(pattern, par_name) {
  c1 <- scan(paste0("pred22_c1_", pattern), quiet = TRUE)
  c2 <- scan(paste0("pred22_c2_", pattern), quiet = TRUE)
  
 
  mcmc.list(
    mcmc(matrix(c1, ncol = 1, dimnames = list(NULL, par_name))),
    mcmc(matrix(c2, ncol = 1, dimnames = list(NULL, par_name)))
  )
}


mu_mcmc  <- load_mcmc_named("mu.dat", "Global_Mean")
u_mcmc   <- load_mcmc_named("ETA_G_varU.dat", "Additive_Variance_G")
e_mcmc   <- load_mcmc_named("varE.dat", "Residual_Variance_E")
env_mcmc <- load_mcmc_named("ETA_ENV_varB.dat","Varianza ambiental")

str(fm_R)

mu_com <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, mu_mcmc))
u_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, u_mcmc))
e_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, e_mcmc))
env_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, env_mcmc))

params_list <- list(mu_mcmc, u_mcmc, e_mcmc,env_mcmc)
file_names   <- c("Trace_Mean", "Trace_VarG", "Trace_VarE", "Trace_VarEnv")


plot_titles <- list(
  expression(mu), 
  expression(sigma[G]^2), 
  expression(sigma[e]^2),
  expression(sigma[env]^2)
)

# =========================================================
# 3. BUCLE PARA GUARDAR TRAZAS HD
# =========================================================
for(i in 1:length(params_list)){
  p <- mcmc_trace(params_list[[i]]) + 
    ggtitle(plot_titles[[i]]) +  # Doble corchete para la expresión
    scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
    theme_classic() + 
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none",
      plot.title = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0.5)
    ) +
    labs(x = "Iteration", y = "Value")
  
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(filename = paste0(file_names[i], ".tiff"), plot = p,
         width = 8, height = 4.5, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
}


```


#HDI

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 5. HDI ANALYSIS (CORRECTED FOR FULL CHAINS)
# =========================================================
library(HDInterval)
mu_real_chain  <- (mu_com ) 
u_real_chain   <- u_com  
e_real_chain   <- e_com 
env_real_chain   <- env_com 

h2_chain <- (mu_com + u_com+env_com ) / (mu_com + u_com + env_com  + e_com)




summary(h2_chain)
# 3. Función de cálculo (Mediana y HDI 95%)
calc_real_stats <- function(chain) {
  h <- hdi(chain, credMass = 0.95)
  return(c(Median = median(chain), Lower = h[1], Upper = h[2]))
}

# 4. Aplicar a las cadenas
summary_table <- rbind(
  `Global Mean (mu)`  = calc_real_stats(mu_real_chain),
  `Variance G`        = calc_real_stats(u_real_chain),
  `Variance E`        = calc_real_stats(e_real_chain),
   `Variance Env`        = calc_real_stats(env_real_chain),
  `Heritability (h2)` = calc_real_stats(h2_chain)
)

colnames(summary_table) <- c("Posterior Median", "95% HDI Lower", "95% HDI Upper")

cat("\n========== CORRECTED POSTERIOR SUMMARY (REAL SCALE) ==========\n")
print(round(summary_table, 4))

summary(h2_chain)
```




# Gelma-Rubin
```{r}
# =========================================================
# 6. GELMAN-RUBIN 
# =========================================================
library(coda)

# Calcular el diagnóstico para cada parámetro
# Un valor cercano a 1.00 (ej. < 1.1) indica buena convergencia
gr_mu  <- gelman.diag(mu_mcmc)
gr_u   <- gelman.diag(u_mcmc)
gr_e   <- gelman.diag(e_mcmc)
gr_env <- gelman.diag(env_mcmc)

gr_table <- rbind(
  `Global Mean (mu)`   = gr_mu$psrf[1, ],
  `Variance G`         = gr_u$psrf[1, ],
  `Variance E`         = gr_e$psrf[1, ],
   `Variance Env`         = gr_env$psrf[1, ]
)

colnames(gr_table) <- c("Point Est. (R-hat)", "Upper C.I.")

cat("\n========== GELMAN-RUBIN DIAGNOSTIC ==========\n")
cat("Values close to 1.00 indicate convergence.\n\n")
print(round(gr_table, 4))
cat("=============================================\n")


max_rhat <- max(gr_table[, 1])
if(max_rhat < 1.1) {
  cat("Success: All parameters have converged (R-hat < 1.1).\n")
} else {
  cat("Warning: Some parameters may not have converged. Consider increasing nIter.\n")
}
```





# 2021

```{r}

Ren <- Dat[Dat$V7 == "Yield.LSU_01.0000138", ]

R_train <- Ren[Ren$V6 %in% c(2018, 2019, 2020, 2022, 2023, 2024), ]
R_test  <- Ren[Ren$V6 == 2021, ]

y_train_raw <- as.numeric(R_train$V5)/1000
y_test_raw  <- as.numeric(R_test$V5)/1000

id_train_raw <- trimws(as.character(R_train$V1))
id_test_raw  <- trimws(as.character(R_test$V1))


keep_train <- id_train_raw %in% rownames(X)
keep_test  <- id_test_raw  %in% rownames(X)

id_train <- id_train_raw[keep_train]
id_test  <- id_test_raw[keep_test]

y_train <- y_train_raw[keep_train]
y_test  <- y_test_raw[keep_test]


X_train <- X[id_train, , drop = FALSE]
X_test  <- X[id_test, , drop = FALSE]


mu_train <- colMeans(X_train)
Xc_train <- scale(X_train, center = mu_train, scale = FALSE)
Xc_test  <- scale(X_test, center = mu_train, scale = FALSE)

# Matriz K entrenamiento
K_train <- tcrossprod(Xc_train) / ncol(Xc_train)
# Matriz K test-train
K_test_train <- (Xc_test %*% t(Xc_train)) / ncol(Xc_train)

# =========================================================
# 4. Ajuste del Modelo BGLR (RKHS)
year_train <- factor(R_train$V6[keep_train])
X_year <- model.matrix(~ 0 + year_train)

ETA <- list(
  ENV = list(X = X_year, model = "BRR"),
  G   = list(K = K_train, model = "RKHS")
)
# =========================================================
#ETA <- list(list(K = K_train, model = "RKHS"))

set.seed(12)
fm_R <- BGLR(y = y_train, ETA = ETA, nIter = 75000, burnIn = 50000, 
             saveAt = "pred21_c1_", verbose = FALSE)

set.seed(13)
fm2_R <- BGLR(y = y_train, ETA = ETA, nIter = 75000, burnIn = 50000, 
              saveAt = "pred21_c2_", verbose = FALSE)

# =========================================================
# 5. Predicción 
# =========================================================
library(MASS)

# Promedio de efectos aleatorios y media global
u_hat_train <- (fm_R$ETA[[2]]$u + fm2_R$ETA[[2]]$u) / 2
mu_hat      <- (fm_R$mu + fm2_R$mu) / 2

# BLUP para el año 2021 (Test)
u_hat_2021      <- K_test_train %*% ginv(K_train) %*% u_hat_train
pred_2021_total <- as.vector(u_hat_2021 + mu_hat)


# =========================================================
# 6. Resultados y Gráfico
# =========================================================
cor_2021 <- cor(y_test, pred_2021_total, use = "complete.obs")
rmse_2021 <- sqrt(mean((y_test - pred_2021_total)^2))

cat("Correlación 2021:", round(cor_2021, 4), "\n")
cat("RMSE 2021:", round(rmse_2021, 4), "\n")

plot(y_test, pred_2021_total, col = "blue", pch = 19,
     xlab = "Observado 2021", ylab = "Predicho GBLUP",
     main = paste("Predicción 2021 - Cor:", round(cor_2021, 3)))
abline(0, 1, col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
```


# Posterior
```{r}
# =========================================================

# =========================================================
library(coda)
library(bayesplot)
library(ggplot2)

cols <- c("#FF0FFF", "#00BFFF", "#32CD32", "#FF0000")

load_mcmc_named <- function(pattern, par_name) {
  c1 <- scan(paste0("pred21_c1_", pattern), quiet = TRUE)
  c2 <- scan(paste0("pred21_c2_", pattern), quiet = TRUE)
  
 
  mcmc.list(
    mcmc(matrix(c1, ncol = 1, dimnames = list(NULL, par_name))),
    mcmc(matrix(c2, ncol = 1, dimnames = list(NULL, par_name)))
  )
}


mu_mcmc  <- load_mcmc_named("mu.dat", "Global_Mean")
u_mcmc   <- load_mcmc_named("ETA_G_varU.dat", "Additive_Variance_G")
e_mcmc   <- load_mcmc_named("varE.dat", "Residual_Variance_E")
env_mcmc <- load_mcmc_named("ETA_ENV_varB.dat","Varianza ambiental")

str(fm_R)

mu_com <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, mu_mcmc))
u_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, u_mcmc))
e_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, e_mcmc))
env_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, env_mcmc))

params_list <- list(mu_mcmc, u_mcmc, e_mcmc,env_mcmc)
file_names   <- c("Trace_Mean", "Trace_VarG", "Trace_VarE", "Trace_VarEnv")


plot_titles <- list(
  expression(mu), 
  expression(sigma[G]^2), 
  expression(sigma[e]^2),
  expression(sigma[env]^2)
)

# =========================================================
# 3. BUCLE PARA GUARDAR TRAZAS HD
# =========================================================
for(i in 1:length(params_list)){
  p <- mcmc_trace(params_list[[i]]) + 
    ggtitle(plot_titles[[i]]) +  # Doble corchete para la expresión
    scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
    theme_classic() + 
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none",
      plot.title = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0.5)
    ) +
    labs(x = "Iteration", y = "Value")
  
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(filename = paste0(file_names[i], ".tiff"), plot = p,
         width = 8, height = 4.5, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
}


```


# HDI

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 5. HDI ANALYSIS (CORRECTED FOR FULL CHAINS)
# =========================================================
library(HDInterval)
mu_real_chain  <- (mu_com ) 
u_real_chain   <- u_com  
e_real_chain   <- e_com 
env_real_chain   <- env_com 

h2_chain <- (mu_com + u_com+env_com ) / (mu_com + u_com + env_com  + e_com)




summary(h2_chain)
# 3. Función de cálculo (Mediana y HDI 95%)
calc_real_stats <- function(chain) {
  h <- hdi(chain, credMass = 0.95)
  return(c(Median = median(chain), Lower = h[1], Upper = h[2]))
}

# 4. Aplicar a las cadenas
summary_table <- rbind(
  `Global Mean (mu)`  = calc_real_stats(mu_real_chain),
  `Variance G`        = calc_real_stats(u_real_chain),
  `Variance E`        = calc_real_stats(e_real_chain),
   `Variance Env`        = calc_real_stats(env_real_chain),
  `Heritability (h2)` = calc_real_stats(h2_chain)
)

colnames(summary_table) <- c("Posterior Median", "95% HDI Lower", "95% HDI Upper")

cat("\n========== CORRECTED POSTERIOR SUMMARY (REAL SCALE) ==========\n")
print(round(summary_table, 4))

summary(h2_chain)
```




# Gelma-Rubin
```{r}
# =========================================================
# 6. GELMAN-RUBIN 
# =========================================================
library(coda)

# Calcular el diagnóstico para cada parámetro
# Un valor cercano a 1.00 (ej. < 1.1) indica buena convergencia
gr_mu  <- gelman.diag(mu_mcmc)
gr_u   <- gelman.diag(u_mcmc)
gr_e   <- gelman.diag(e_mcmc)
gr_env <- gelman.diag(env_mcmc)

gr_table <- rbind(
  `Global Mean (mu)`   = gr_mu$psrf[1, ],
  `Variance G`         = gr_u$psrf[1, ],
  `Variance E`         = gr_e$psrf[1, ],
   `Variance Env`         = gr_env$psrf[1, ]
)

colnames(gr_table) <- c("Point Est. (R-hat)", "Upper C.I.")

cat("\n========== GELMAN-RUBIN DIAGNOSTIC ==========\n")
cat("Values close to 1.00 indicate convergence.\n\n")
print(round(gr_table, 4))
cat("=============================================\n")


max_rhat <- max(gr_table[, 1])
if(max_rhat < 1.1) {
  cat("Success: All parameters have converged (R-hat < 1.1).\n")
} else {
  cat("Warning: Some parameters may not have converged. Consider increasing nIter.\n")
}
```



# 2020


```{r}
Ren <- Dat[Dat$V7 == "Yield.LSU_01.0000138", ]


R_train <- Ren[Ren$V6 %in% c(2018, 2019, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024), ]

R_test  <- Ren[Ren$V6 == 2020, ]


y_train_raw <- as.numeric(R_train$V5)/1000
y_test_raw  <- as.numeric(R_test$V5)/1000

id_train_raw <- trimws(as.character(R_train$V1))
id_test_raw  <- trimws(as.character(R_test$V1))


keep_train <- id_train_raw %in% rownames(X)
keep_test  <- id_test_raw  %in% rownames(X)

id_train <- id_train_raw[keep_train]
id_test  <- id_test_raw[keep_test]

y_train <- y_train_raw[keep_train]
y_test  <- y_test_raw[keep_test]


X_train <- X[id_train, , drop = FALSE]
X_test  <- X[id_test, , drop = FALSE]


mu_train <- colMeans(X_train)
Xc_train <- scale(X_train, center = mu_train, scale = FALSE)
Xc_test  <- scale(X_test, center = mu_train, scale = FALSE)

K_train <- tcrossprod(Xc_train) / ncol(Xc_train)

K_test_train <- (Xc_test %*% t(Xc_train)) / ncol(Xc_train)

# =========================================================
# 4. Ajuste del Modelo BGLR (RKHS)
year_train <- factor(R_train$V6[keep_train])
X_year <- model.matrix(~ 0 + year_train)

ETA <- list(
  ENV = list(X = X_year, model = "BRR"),
  G   = list(K = K_train, model = "RKHS")
)
# =========================================================
#ETA <- list(list(K = K_train, model = "RKHS"))

set.seed(12)
fm_R <- BGLR(y = y_train, ETA = ETA, nIter = 75000, burnIn = 50000, 
             saveAt = "pred20_c1_", verbose = FALSE)

set.seed(13)
fm2_R <- BGLR(y = y_train, ETA = ETA, nIter = 75000, burnIn = 50000, 
              saveAt = "pred20_c2_", verbose = FALSE)


library(MASS)

u_hat_train <- (fm_R$ETA[[2]]$u + fm2_R$ETA[[2]]$u) / 2
mu_hat      <- (fm_R$mu + fm2_R$mu) / 2


u_hat_2020      <- K_test_train %*% ginv(K_train) %*% u_hat_train
pred_2020_total <- as.vector(u_hat_2020 + mu_hat)



# =========================================================
cor_2020 <- cor(y_test, pred_2020_total, use = "complete.obs")
rmse_2020 <- sqrt(mean((y_test - pred_2020_total)^2))

cat("Correlación 2020:", round(cor_2020, 4), "\n")
cat("RMSE 2020:", round(rmse_2020, 4), "\n")


```

# Posterior
```{r}
# =========================================================

# =========================================================
library(coda)
library(bayesplot)
library(ggplot2)

cols <- c("#FF0FFF", "#00BFFF", "#32CD32", "#FF0000")

load_mcmc_named <- function(pattern, par_name) {
  c1 <- scan(paste0("pred20_c1_", pattern), quiet = TRUE)
  c2 <- scan(paste0("pred20_c2_", pattern), quiet = TRUE)
  
 
  mcmc.list(
    mcmc(matrix(c1, ncol = 1, dimnames = list(NULL, par_name))),
    mcmc(matrix(c2, ncol = 1, dimnames = list(NULL, par_name)))
  )
}


mu_mcmc  <- load_mcmc_named("mu.dat", "Global_Mean")
u_mcmc   <- load_mcmc_named("ETA_G_varU.dat", "Additive_Variance_G")
e_mcmc   <- load_mcmc_named("varE.dat", "Residual_Variance_E")
env_mcmc <- load_mcmc_named("ETA_ENV_varB.dat","Varianza ambiental")

str(fm_R)

mu_com <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, mu_mcmc))
u_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, u_mcmc))
e_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, e_mcmc))
env_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, env_mcmc))

params_list <- list(mu_mcmc, u_mcmc, e_mcmc,env_mcmc)
file_names   <- c("Trace_Mean", "Trace_VarG", "Trace_VarE", "Trace_VarEnv")


plot_titles <- list(
  expression(mu), 
  expression(sigma[G]^2), 
  expression(sigma[e]^2),
  expression(sigma[env]^2)
)

# =========================================================
# 3. BUCLE PARA GUARDAR TRAZAS HD
# =========================================================
for(i in 1:length(params_list)){
  p <- mcmc_trace(params_list[[i]]) + 
    ggtitle(plot_titles[[i]]) +  # Doble corchete para la expresión
    scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
    theme_classic() + 
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none",
      plot.title = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0.5)
    ) +
    labs(x = "Iteration", y = "Value")
  
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(filename = paste0(file_names[i], ".tiff"), plot = p,
         width = 8, height = 4.5, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
}


```


# HDI

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 5. HDI ANALYSIS (CORRECTED FOR FULL CHAINS)
# =========================================================
library(HDInterval)
mu_real_chain  <- (mu_com ) 
u_real_chain   <- u_com  
e_real_chain   <- e_com 
env_real_chain   <- env_com 

h2_chain <- (mu_com + u_com+env_com ) / (mu_com + u_com + env_com  + e_com)




summary(h2_chain)
# 3. Función de cálculo (Mediana y HDI 95%)
calc_real_stats <- function(chain) {
  h <- hdi(chain, credMass = 0.95)
  return(c(Median = median(chain), Lower = h[1], Upper = h[2]))
}

# 4. Aplicar a las cadenas
summary_table <- rbind(
  `Global Mean (mu)`  = calc_real_stats(mu_real_chain),
  `Variance G`        = calc_real_stats(u_real_chain),
  `Variance E`        = calc_real_stats(e_real_chain),
   `Variance Env`        = calc_real_stats(env_real_chain),
  `Heritability (h2)` = calc_real_stats(h2_chain)
)

colnames(summary_table) <- c("Posterior Median", "95% HDI Lower", "95% HDI Upper")

cat("\n========== CORRECTED POSTERIOR SUMMARY (REAL SCALE) ==========\n")
print(round(summary_table, 4))

summary(h2_chain)
```




# Gelma-Rubin
```{r}
# =========================================================
# 6. GELMAN-RUBIN 
# =========================================================
library(coda)

# Calcular el diagnóstico para cada parámetro
# Un valor cercano a 1.00 (ej. < 1.1) indica buena convergencia
gr_mu  <- gelman.diag(mu_mcmc)
gr_u   <- gelman.diag(u_mcmc)
gr_e   <- gelman.diag(e_mcmc)
gr_env <- gelman.diag(env_mcmc)

gr_table <- rbind(
  `Global Mean (mu)`   = gr_mu$psrf[1, ],
  `Variance G`         = gr_u$psrf[1, ],
  `Variance E`         = gr_e$psrf[1, ],
   `Variance Env`         = gr_env$psrf[1, ]
)

colnames(gr_table) <- c("Point Est. (R-hat)", "Upper C.I.")

cat("\n========== GELMAN-RUBIN DIAGNOSTIC ==========\n")
cat("Values close to 1.00 indicate convergence.\n\n")
print(round(gr_table, 4))
cat("=============================================\n")


max_rhat <- max(gr_table[, 1])
if(max_rhat < 1.1) {
  cat("Success: All parameters have converged (R-hat < 1.1).\n")
} else {
  cat("Warning: Some parameters may not have converged. Consider increasing nIter.\n")
}
```


# 19

```{r}
Ren <- Dat[Dat$V7 == "Yield.LSU_01.0000138", ]

R_train <- Ren[Ren$V6 %in% c(2018, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024), ]

R_test  <- Ren[Ren$V6==2019,]

y_train_raw <- as.numeric(R_train$V5)/1000
y_test_raw  <- as.numeric(R_test$V5)/1000

id_train_raw <- trimws(as.character(R_train$V1))
id_test_raw  <- trimws(as.character(R_test$V1))

keep_train <- id_train_raw %in% rownames(X)
keep_test  <- id_test_raw  %in% rownames(X)

id_train <- id_train_raw[keep_train]
id_test  <- id_test_raw[keep_test]

y_train <- y_train_raw[keep_train]
y_test  <- y_test_raw[keep_test]

X_train <- X[id_train, , drop = FALSE]
X_test  <- X[id_test, , drop = FALSE]


mu_train <- colMeans(X_train)
Xc_train <- scale(X_train, center = mu_train, scale = FALSE)
Xc_test  <- scale(X_test, center = mu_train, scale = FALSE)


K_train <- tcrossprod(Xc_train) / ncol(Xc_train)

K_test_train <- (Xc_test %*% t(Xc_train)) / ncol(Xc_train)

year_train <- factor(R_train$V6[keep_train])
X_year <- model.matrix(~ 0 + year_train)

ETA <- list(
  ENV = list(X = X_year, model = "BRR"),
  G   = list(K = K_train, model = "RKHS")
)
#ETA <- list(list(K = K_train, model = "RKHS"))

set.seed(12)
fm_R <- BGLR(y = y_train, ETA = ETA, nIter = 75000, burnIn = 50000, 
             saveAt = "pred19_c1_", verbose = FALSE)

set.seed(13)
fm2_R <- BGLR(y = y_train, ETA = ETA, nIter = 75000, burnIn = 50000, 
              saveAt = "pred19_c2_", verbose = FALSE)

library(MASS)

u_hat_train <- (fm_R$ETA[[2]]$u + fm2_R$ETA[[2]]$u) / 2
mu_hat      <- (fm_R$mu + fm2_R$mu) / 2

u_hat_2019      <- K_test_train %*% ginv(K_train) %*% u_hat_train
pred_2019_total <- as.vector(u_hat_2019 + mu_hat)



cor_2019 <- cor(y_test, pred_2019_total, use = "complete.obs")
rmse_2019 <- sqrt(mean((y_test - pred_2019_total)^2))

cat("Correlación 2019:", round(cor_2019, 4), "\n")
cat("RMSE 2019:", round(rmse_2019, 4), "\n")


```


# Posterior
```{r}
# =========================================================

# =========================================================
library(coda)
library(bayesplot)
library(ggplot2)

cols <- c("#FF0FFF", "#00BFFF", "#32CD32", "#FF0000")

load_mcmc_named <- function(pattern, par_name) {
  c1 <- scan(paste0("pred19_c1_", pattern), quiet = TRUE)
  c2 <- scan(paste0("pred19_c2_", pattern), quiet = TRUE)
  
 
  mcmc.list(
    mcmc(matrix(c1, ncol = 1, dimnames = list(NULL, par_name))),
    mcmc(matrix(c2, ncol = 1, dimnames = list(NULL, par_name)))
  )
}


mu_mcmc  <- load_mcmc_named("mu.dat", "Global_Mean")
u_mcmc   <- load_mcmc_named("ETA_G_varU.dat", "Additive_Variance_G")
e_mcmc   <- load_mcmc_named("varE.dat", "Residual_Variance_E")
env_mcmc <- load_mcmc_named("ETA_ENV_varB.dat","Varianza ambiental")

str(fm_R)

mu_com <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, mu_mcmc))
u_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, u_mcmc))
e_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, e_mcmc))
env_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, env_mcmc))

params_list <- list(mu_mcmc, u_mcmc, e_mcmc,env_mcmc)
file_names   <- c("Trace_Mean", "Trace_VarG", "Trace_VarE", "Trace_VarEnv")


plot_titles <- list(
  expression(mu), 
  expression(sigma[G]^2), 
  expression(sigma[e]^2),
  expression(sigma[env]^2)
)

# =========================================================
# 3. BUCLE PARA GUARDAR TRAZAS HD
# =========================================================
for(i in 1:length(params_list)){
  p <- mcmc_trace(params_list[[i]]) + 
    ggtitle(plot_titles[[i]]) +  # Doble corchete para la expresión
    scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
    theme_classic() + 
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none",
      plot.title = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0.5)
    ) +
    labs(x = "Iteration", y = "Value")
  
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(filename = paste0(file_names[i], ".tiff"), plot = p,
         width = 8, height = 4.5, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
}


```


# HDI

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 5. HDI ANALYSIS (CORRECTED FOR FULL CHAINS)
# =========================================================
library(HDInterval)
mu_real_chain  <- (mu_com ) 
u_real_chain   <- u_com  
e_real_chain   <- e_com 
env_real_chain   <- env_com 

h2_chain <- (mu_com + u_com+env_com ) / (mu_com + u_com + env_com  + e_com)




summary(h2_chain)
# 3. Función de cálculo (Mediana y HDI 95%)
calc_real_stats <- function(chain) {
  h <- hdi(chain, credMass = 0.95)
  return(c(Median = median(chain), Lower = h[1], Upper = h[2]))
}

# 4. Aplicar a las cadenas
summary_table <- rbind(
  `Global Mean (mu)`  = calc_real_stats(mu_real_chain),
  `Variance G`        = calc_real_stats(u_real_chain),
  `Variance E`        = calc_real_stats(e_real_chain),
   `Variance Env`        = calc_real_stats(env_real_chain),
  `Heritability (h2)` = calc_real_stats(h2_chain)
)

colnames(summary_table) <- c("Posterior Median", "95% HDI Lower", "95% HDI Upper")

cat("\n========== CORRECTED POSTERIOR SUMMARY (REAL SCALE) ==========\n")
print(round(summary_table, 4))

summary(h2_chain)
```




# Gelma-Rubin
```{r}
# =========================================================
# 6. GELMAN-RUBIN 
# =========================================================
library(coda)

# Calcular el diagnóstico para cada parámetro
# Un valor cercano a 1.00 (ej. < 1.1) indica buena convergencia
gr_mu  <- gelman.diag(mu_mcmc)
gr_u   <- gelman.diag(u_mcmc)
gr_e   <- gelman.diag(e_mcmc)
gr_env <- gelman.diag(env_mcmc)

gr_table <- rbind(
  `Global Mean (mu)`   = gr_mu$psrf[1, ],
  `Variance G`         = gr_u$psrf[1, ],
  `Variance E`         = gr_e$psrf[1, ],
   `Variance Env`         = gr_env$psrf[1, ]
)

colnames(gr_table) <- c("Point Est. (R-hat)", "Upper C.I.")

cat("\n========== GELMAN-RUBIN DIAGNOSTIC ==========\n")
cat("Values close to 1.00 indicate convergence.\n\n")
print(round(gr_table, 4))
cat("=============================================\n")


max_rhat <- max(gr_table[, 1])
if(max_rhat < 1.1) {
  cat("Success: All parameters have converged (R-hat < 1.1).\n")
} else {
  cat("Warning: Some parameters may not have converged. Consider increasing nIter.\n")
}
```







# 2018

```{r}
Ren <- Dat[Dat$V7 == "Yield.LSU_01.0000138", ]
R_train <- Ren[Ren$V6 %in% c(2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024), ]

R_test  <- Ren[Ren$V6==2018,]

y_train_raw <- as.numeric(R_train$V5)/1000
y_test_raw  <- as.numeric(R_test$V5)/1000

id_train_raw <- trimws(as.character(R_train$V1))
id_test_raw  <- trimws(as.character(R_test$V1))

keep_train <- id_train_raw %in% rownames(X)
keep_test  <- id_test_raw  %in% rownames(X)

id_train <- id_train_raw[keep_train]
id_test  <- id_test_raw[keep_test]

y_train <- y_train_raw[keep_train]
y_test  <- y_test_raw[keep_test]

X_train <- X[id_train, , drop = FALSE]
X_test  <- X[id_test, , drop = FALSE]

mu_train <- colMeans(X_train)
Xc_train <- scale(X_train, center = mu_train, scale = FALSE)
Xc_test  <- scale(X_test, center = mu_train, scale = FALSE)


K_train <- tcrossprod(Xc_train) / ncol(Xc_train)

K_test_train <- (Xc_test %*% t(Xc_train)) / ncol(Xc_train)

##########################################
year_train <- factor(R_train$V6[keep_train])
X_year <- model.matrix(~ 0 + year_train)

ETA <- list(
  ENV = list(X = X_year, model = "BRR"),
  G   = list(K = K_train, model = "RKHS")
)
#ETA <- list(list(K = K_train, model = "RKHS"))

set.seed(12)
fm_R <- BGLR(y = y_train, ETA = ETA, nIter = 75000, burnIn = 55000, 
             saveAt = "pred18_c1_", verbose = FALSE)

set.seed(13)
fm2_R <- BGLR(y = y_train, ETA = ETA, nIter = 75000, burnIn = 55000, 
              saveAt = "pred18_c2_", verbose = FALSE)

library(MASS)

u_hat_train <- (fm_R$ETA[[2]]$u + fm2_R$ETA[[2]]$u) / 2
mu_hat  <- (fm_R$mu + fm2_R$mu) / 2


u_hat_2018      <- K_test_train %*% ginv(K_train) %*% u_hat_train
pred_2018_total <- as.vector(u_hat_2018 + mu_hat)


cor_2018 <- cor(y_test, pred_2018_total, use = "complete.obs")
rmse_2018 <- sqrt(mean((y_test - pred_2018_total)^2))

cat("Correlación 2018:", round(cor_2018, 4), "\n")
cat("RMSE 2018:", round(rmse_2018, 4), "\n")


```


# Posterior
```{r}
# =========================================================

# =========================================================
library(coda)
library(bayesplot)
library(ggplot2)

cols <- c("#FF0FFF", "#00BFFF", "#32CD32", "#FF0000")

load_mcmc_named <- function(pattern, par_name) {
  c1 <- scan(paste0("pred18_c1_", pattern), quiet = TRUE)
  c2 <- scan(paste0("pred18_c2_", pattern), quiet = TRUE)
  
 
  mcmc.list(
    mcmc(matrix(c1, ncol = 1, dimnames = list(NULL, par_name))),
    mcmc(matrix(c2, ncol = 1, dimnames = list(NULL, par_name)))
  )
}


mu_mcmc  <- load_mcmc_named("mu.dat", "Global_Mean")
u_mcmc   <- load_mcmc_named("ETA_G_varU.dat", "Additive_Variance_G")
e_mcmc   <- load_mcmc_named("varE.dat", "Residual_Variance_E")
env_mcmc <- load_mcmc_named("ETA_ENV_varB.dat","Varianza ambiental")

str(fm_R)

mu_com <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, mu_mcmc))
u_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, u_mcmc))
e_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, e_mcmc))
env_com  <- as.numeric(do.call(rbind, env_mcmc))

params_list <- list(mu_mcmc, u_mcmc, e_mcmc,env_mcmc)
file_names   <- c("Trace_Mean", "Trace_VarG", "Trace_VarE", "Trace_VarEnv")


plot_titles <- list(
  expression(mu), 
  expression(sigma[G]^2), 
  expression(sigma[e]^2),
  expression(sigma[env]^2)
)

# =========================================================
# 3. BUCLE PARA GUARDAR TRAZAS HD
# =========================================================
for(i in 1:length(params_list)){
  p <- mcmc_trace(params_list[[i]]) + 
    ggtitle(plot_titles[[i]]) +  # Doble corchete para la expresión
    scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
    theme_classic() + 
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none",
      plot.title = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0.5)
    ) +
    labs(x = "Iteration", y = "Value")
  
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(filename = paste0(file_names[i], ".tiff"), plot = p,
         width = 8, height = 4.5, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
}


```


#HDI

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 5. HDI ANALYSIS (CORRECTED FOR FULL CHAINS)
# =========================================================
library(HDInterval)
mu_real_chain  <- (mu_com ) 
u_real_chain   <- u_com  
e_real_chain   <- e_com 
env_real_chain   <- env_com 

h2_chain <- (mu_com + u_com+env_com ) / (mu_com + u_com + env_com  + e_com)




summary(h2_chain)
# 3. Función de cálculo (Mediana y HDI 95%)
calc_real_stats <- function(chain) {
  h <- hdi(chain, credMass = 0.95)
  return(c(Median = median(chain), Lower = h[1], Upper = h[2]))
}

# 4. Aplicar a las cadenas
summary_table <- rbind(
  `Global Mean (mu)`  = calc_real_stats(mu_real_chain),
  `Variance G`        = calc_real_stats(u_real_chain),
  `Variance E`        = calc_real_stats(e_real_chain),
   `Variance Env`        = calc_real_stats(env_real_chain),
  `Heritability (h2)` = calc_real_stats(h2_chain)
)

colnames(summary_table) <- c("Posterior Median", "95% HDI Lower", "95% HDI Upper")

cat("\n========== CORRECTED POSTERIOR SUMMARY (REAL SCALE) ==========\n")
print(round(summary_table, 4))

summary(h2_chain)
```




# Gelma-Rubin
```{r}
# =========================================================
# 6. GELMAN-RUBIN 
# =========================================================
library(coda)

# Calcular el diagnóstico para cada parámetro
# Un valor cercano a 1.00 (ej. < 1.1) indica buena convergencia
gr_mu  <- gelman.diag(mu_mcmc)
gr_u   <- gelman.diag(u_mcmc)
gr_e   <- gelman.diag(e_mcmc)
gr_env <- gelman.diag(env_mcmc)

gr_table <- rbind(
  `Global Mean (mu)`   = gr_mu$psrf[1, ],
  `Variance G`         = gr_u$psrf[1, ],
  `Variance E`         = gr_e$psrf[1, ],
   `Variance Env`         = gr_env$psrf[1, ]
)

colnames(gr_table) <- c("Point Est. (R-hat)", "Upper C.I.")

cat("\n========== GELMAN-RUBIN DIAGNOSTIC ==========\n")
cat("Values close to 1.00 indicate convergence.\n\n")
print(round(gr_table, 4))
cat("=============================================\n")


max_rhat <- max(gr_table[, 1])
if(max_rhat < 1.1) {
  cat("Success: All parameters have converged (R-hat < 1.1).\n")
} else {
  cat("Warning: Some parameters may not have converged. Consider increasing nIter.\n")
}
```
